description_long: This cowshed features a traditional gable-roofed main structure with a reddish-brown tiled roof and a large open entrance on one side. Attached to the main building is a covered extension with a flat, corrugated metal roof supported by wooden posts, providing a sheltered area likely used for livestock or storage. The main building is constructed from white masonry or concrete walls, with two rectangular window openings on the longer side and a wide open doorway on the front. The ground is paved with reddish-brown bricks, extending under both the main building and the covered extension. The structure is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements, focusing on function and durability. The overall design is typical of rural or peri-urban agricultural buildings, blending robust materials with open, accessible spaces for animal movement and ventilation.
description: A traditional cowshed with a gable-tiled roof, white masonry walls, and a large open entrance, featuring a side extension with a corrugated metal roof supported by wooden posts.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main gable roof, open entrance, and the attached covered extension with a metal roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the corrugated metal roof extension and the supporting wooden posts, with the gable roof sloping away.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying two rectangular window openings in the white masonry wall and the underside of the gable roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the full extent of the brick-paved ground and the intersection of the two roof types.
attribute_roof_type: gable with attached flat (shed) roof extension
attribute_door_style: open rectangular entrance, no visible door
attribute_window_presence: two rectangular windows on the side wall
